panel.origin: A sweet, slightly floral and fruity aromatic with a slightly green and dried plant character.
panel.mixology: A gentle, calming floral note resembling dried chamomile flowers or herbal tea.

panel.origin: A sweet, complex floral aromatic associated with fresh roses.
panel.mixology: A romantic, highly perfumed floral scent exactly like a blooming rose bush.

panel.origin: An intense, slightly pungent, sweet floral aromatic associated with jasmine flowers. A hallmark of washed Ethiopian Yirgacheffes.
panel.mixology: A highly aromatic, sweet and elegant floral note, similar to jasmine green tea.

panel.origin: A somewhat brown, musty, dried plant and slightly astringent aromatic associated with black tea.
panel.mixology: The distinct, slightly dry and earthy aroma of steeped black tea leaves.

panel.origin: A sweet, dark, slightly tart and musty aromatic associated with blackberries.
panel.mixology: A dark, rich berry flavor with a slight tartness, like wild blackberry jam.

panel.origin: A sweet, slightly sour and floral aromatic associated with fresh raspberries.
panel.mixology: A bright, zesty, and sweet red berry flavor.

panel.origin: A sweet, dark, slightly dusty aromatic associated with blueberries. Highly prevalent in natural-processed Ethiopian coffees.
panel.mixology: A sweet, dark berry note that often reminds people of blueberry muffins or pie.

panel.origin: A sweet, somewhat fruity and slightly floral aromatic associated with fresh strawberries.
panel.mixology: A soft, sweet, and juicy red berry flavor.

panel.origin: A sweet, brown, slightly fruity and musty aromatic associated with dried grapes.
panel.mixology: A concentrated, dark sweetness exactly like a handful of raisins.

panel.origin: A sweet, brown, slightly floral aromatic associated with dried plums.
panel.mixology: A deep, slightly sticky and rich fruit flavor, similar to plum pudding.

panel.origin: A sweet, rich, slightly woody and nutty aromatic associated with coconut meat.
panel.mixology: A sweet, creamy, and tropical flavor resembling shredded coconut.

panel.origin: A sour, fruity, slightly floral and bitter aromatic associated with red cherries.
panel.mixology: A bright, tart red fruit flavor, similar to fresh cherries or cherry juice.

panel.origin: A sour, sweet, slightly dark fruity aromatic associated with pomegranate seeds.
panel.mixology: A very tart, tangy, and bright dark fruit flavor.

panel.origin: A sweet, slightly sour, fruity aromatic associated with fresh pineapple.
panel.mixology: A vibrant, sweet, and sharply tangy tropical fruit note.

panel.origin: A sweet, fruity, slightly floral and musty aromatic associated with grapes.
panel.mixology: A juicy, sweet flavor reminiscent of fresh white or red table grapes.

panel.origin: A sweet, somewhat sour, fruity aromatic associated with fresh apples (malic acid).
panel.mixology: A crisp, refreshing fruitiness like biting into a fresh green or red apple.

panel.origin: A sweet, slightly floral and fruity aromatic associated with fresh peaches.
panel.mixology: A soft, sweet, and highly aromatic stone fruit flavor.

panel.origin: A sweet, slightly floral and musty aromatic associated with fresh pears.
panel.mixology: A mild, sweet, and slightly grainy fruit flavor like a ripe pear.

panel.origin: A sour, slightly bitter, and sweet citrus aromatic. Often linked to naringin presence.
panel.mixology: A sharp, bright citrus flavor with a distinct bitter edge, like ruby red grapefruit.

panel.origin: A sweet, slightly sour, and floral citrus aromatic.
panel.mixology: A sweet, juicy, and accessible citrus flavor like a freshly peeled orange.

panel.origin: A sour, slightly sweet, and pungent citrus aromatic (citric acid).
panel.mixology: A highly acidic, bright, and zesty citrus note.

panel.origin: A sour, slightly sweet, green, and pungent citrus aromatic.
panel.mixology: A tart, green, and snappy citrus flavor, sharper than lemon.

panel.origin: An aromatic associated with the impression of a sour product.
panel.mixology: A general tangy or tart smell that makes your mouth water before you even sip.

panel.origin: A sour, sharp, pungent aromatic associated with vinegar. Suggests over-fermentation in processing.
panel.mixology: A sharp, biting sourness exactly like white vinegar.

panel.origin: A sour, fermented-dairy, slightly sweaty aromatic. A severe processing defect.
panel.mixology: An unpleasant sourness reminiscent of rancid butter or baby vomit.

panel.origin: A sweaty, cheesy, fermented aromatic. Often indicates degraded coffee or processing flaws.
panel.mixology: A harsh, funky smell that resembles aged cheese or sweaty socks.

panel.origin: A clean, sour, sharp taste associated with citrus fruits.
panel.mixology: A bright, snappy tartness that gives the coffee a refreshing, lemonade-like zing.

panel.origin: A clean, sour, and tart taste associated with green apples.
panel.mixology: A crisp, lingering tartness similar to biting into a Granny Smith apple.

panel.origin: A sharp, pungent, somewhat fruity and alcohol-like aromatic. Often found in natural processed coffees.
panel.mixology: A rich, complex flavor that reminds you of dry red wine.

panel.origin: A sharp, pungent, alcoholic, brown, and somewhat grainy aromatic.
panel.mixology: A deep, boozy, and slightly woody flavor reminiscent of bourbon or whiskey.

panel.origin: A sharp, pungent, somewhat sour and fruity aromatic. Can cross into defect territory if over-expressed.
panel.mixology: A funky, sour-fruit flavor like kombucha or over-fermented fruit mash.

panel.origin: A sweet, slightly sour, and musty aromatic associated with fruit past its optimal ripeness.
panel.mixology: A heavy, intense sweetness like a banana or peach that is entirely brown and soft.

panel.origin: A green, astringent, slightly sour aromatic associated with unripe fruit.
panel.mixology: A tart, drying sensation like biting into a green, unready piece of fruit.

panel.origin: A green, sweet, and slightly earthy aromatic associated with fresh snow peas.
panel.mixology: A sweet, crisp, and refreshing green vegetable flavor.

panel.origin: A green, slightly sweet aromatic associated with freshly cut plant material.
panel.mixology: A bright, clean, and vibrant aroma like a newly cut stem or fresh greens.

panel.origin: A pungent, bitter, slightly brown aromatic associated with cooked green vegetables (kale/spinach).
panel.mixology: A heavy, earthy, and slightly bitter vegetable flavor like cooked spinach.

panel.origin: A general green, slightly musty aromatic associated with plant material.
panel.mixology: A general plant-like flavor, similar to celery or plant stems.

panel.origin: A dry, green, slightly brown and musty aromatic associated with dried grasses.
panel.mixology: A dry, rustic flavor reminiscent of a barn or dried summer grass.

panel.origin: A green, somewhat pungent, and slightly sweet aromatic associated with culinary herbs.
panel.mixology: A savory, aromatic quality that reminds you of basil, oregano, or mint.

panel.origin: A green, slightly spicy, and fruity aromatic associated with olive oil.
panel.mixology: A rich, slightly savory and slick flavor resembling extra virgin olive oil.

panel.origin: A green, musty, and somewhat sweet aromatic associated with uncooked plant material.
panel.mixology: A harsh, uncooked flavor similar to raw nuts or raw grains.

panel.origin: A green, slightly musty and earthy aromatic associated with legumes/beans.
panel.mixology: A starchy, raw vegetable flavor like unroasted green beans.

panel.origin: A brown, slightly musty and cardboard-like aromatic associated with aged/oxidized coffee.
panel.mixology: A flat, lifeless flavor indicating the coffee has been sitting out too long.

panel.origin: A brown, slightly papery and woody aromatic. Often linked to old green coffee or poor storage.
panel.mixology: Tastes literally like chewing on a piece of brown cardboard.

panel.origin: A slightly brown and musty aromatic. Can be introduced by paper filters during brewing.
panel.mixology: A dry, blank flavor like a paper bag or a dry paper filter.

panel.origin: A brown, musty, and somewhat sweet aromatic associated with dry wood (Past Crop green defect).
panel.mixology: An earthy, dry flavor like pencil shavings or a cedar plank.

panel.origin: A musty, damp, and earthy aromatic associated with mold. A major green coffee defect.
panel.mixology: An unpleasant, wet-basement or damp-towel smell.

panel.origin: A dry, powdery, musty aromatic. Often from poorly stored green coffee.
panel.mixology: A dry, old smell like opening an antique book or a dusty attic.

panel.origin: A musty, somewhat brown aromatic associated with damp soil (often found in wet-hulled Sumatran coffees).
panel.mixology: A deep, heavy flavor exactly like fresh potting soil or a forest floor.

panel.origin: A somewhat sweet, musty, pungent aromatic associated with animal fur or sweat.
panel.mixology: A musky, barnyard-like aroma.

panel.origin: A brown, savory, somewhat salty and musty aromatic associated with boiled meat.
panel.mixology: A savory, umami-rich flavor like beef bouillon or beef stew.

panel.origin: A pungent, somewhat chemical, medicinal aromatic (e.g., Rioy defect).
panel.mixology: A harsh, chemical smell reminiscent of throat lozenges or iodine.

panel.origin: A basic taste characterized by solutions of caffeine or quinine.
panel.mixology: A harsh, biting sensation on the back of the tongue, like black coffee left on a burner.

panel.origin: A basic taste characterized by solutions of sodium chloride.
panel.mixology: A strange, briny taste, rarely found in good coffee unless the water is overly hard.

panel.origin: A pungent, chemical, and somewhat sweet aromatic associated with hospitals/medicine.
panel.mixology: An artificial, clean chemical smell like a dentist's office or band-aids.

panel.origin: A pungent, chemical aromatic associated with crude oil/gasoline.
panel.mixology: A harsh, unpalatable industrial smell like motor oil or diesel.

panel.origin: A pungent, somewhat sulfuric and chemical aromatic.
panel.mixology: A harsh, defensive smell exactly like a skunk or strong cannabis.

panel.origin: A dark, somewhat pungent and chemical aromatic associated with burnt tires or rubber bands.
panel.mixology: An acrid, synthetic smell like a hot car tire or rubber bands.

panel.origin: A harsh, sharp, pungent, and bitter aromatic associated with burnt organic matter.
panel.mixology: A harsh, biting ash flavor that irritates the back of the throat.

panel.origin: A dry, musty, and somewhat brown aromatic associated with the residue of a fire.
panel.mixology: A dry, dusty flavor exactly like cold fireplace ash.

panel.origin: A brown, pungent, somewhat chemical aromatic associated with wood smoke. Common in French roasts.
panel.mixology: A campfire-like flavor, very common in dark, oily roasted coffees.

panel.origin: A dark brown, slightly sweet and musty aromatic associated with heavily roasted foods.
panel.mixology: The standard, deep 'coffee' flavor you get from a dark roast.

panel.origin: A brown, somewhat sweet and musty aromatic associated with whole grains.
panel.mixology: A starchy, comforting flavor like whole wheat bread or bran.

panel.origin: A brown, sweet, slightly musty aromatic associated with roasted malt.
panel.mixology: A sweet, rich, and slightly toasted flavor like malted milk balls or Grape-Nuts.

panel.origin: A sweet, somewhat brown, spicy and musty aromatic associated with cured tobacco.
panel.mixology: A sweet, rich, and fragrant aroma like an unlit cigar or pipe tobacco.

panel.origin: A brown, somewhat musty and pungent aromatic associated with dried tobacco leaves.
panel.mixology: A dry, earthy, and slightly sharp leaf flavor.

panel.origin: A sweet, slightly pungent and floral aromatic associated with anise seed.
panel.mixology: A sweet, sharp spice flavor identical to black licorice.

panel.origin: A sweet, brown, slightly pungent and woody aromatic associated with ground nutmeg.
panel.mixology: A warm, sweet, and slightly woody holiday spice.

panel.origin: A sweet, brown, slightly pungent and woody aromatic associated with cinnamon bark.
panel.mixology: A warm, sweet, and highly aromatic baking spice.

panel.origin: A sweet, pungent, brown and slightly floral aromatic associated with cloves (eugenol).
panel.mixology: A very intense, sharp, and slightly medicinal warm spice.

panel.origin: A sharp, penetrating, and biting aromatic sensation in the nasal cavity.
panel.mixology: A sharp, aggressive spice that tingles the nose, like strong mustard.

panel.origin: A spicy, somewhat woody and pungent aromatic associated with black pepper.
panel.mixology: A sharp, earthy spice exactly like freshly cracked black peppercorns.

panel.origin: A sweet, brown, slightly musty aromatic associated with roasted peanuts.
panel.mixology: A rich, oily nut flavor exactly like roasted peanuts or peanut butter.

panel.origin: A sweet, brown, woody and slightly floral aromatic associated with hazelnuts.
panel.mixology: A rich, sweet, and fragrant nut flavor, like Nutella without the chocolate.

panel.origin: A sweet, slightly brown, floral and musty aromatic associated with almonds (benzaldehyde).
panel.mixology: A sweet, slightly dry nut flavor similar to marzipan.

panel.origin: A sweet, brown, slightly roasted and musty aromatic associated with milk chocolate.
panel.mixology: A sweet, rich, and creamy milk chocolate flavor.

panel.origin: A high-intensity, slightly bitter, roasted and brown aromatic associated with dark chocolate.
panel.mixology: A deep, bittersweet, and intense cacao flavor.

panel.origin: A dark, somewhat bitter, brown and sweet aromatic associated with molasses.
panel.mixology: A dark, heavy, and slightly bitter sweetness like treacle.

panel.origin: A sweet, brown, slightly woody aromatic associated with maple syrup.
panel.mixology: A rich, sticky, and fragrant sweetness exactly like pancake syrup.

panel.origin: A sweet, brown, slightly burnt aromatic associated with heated sugar.
panel.mixology: A rich, deep sweetness like caramel sauce or flan.

panel.origin: A sweet, slightly floral and brown aromatic associated with liquid honey.
panel.mixology: A thick, floral, and natural sweetness.

panel.origin: A sweet, somewhat woody and floral aromatic associated with vanilla beans.
panel.mixology: A rich, complex, and creamy baking spice sweetness.

panel.origin: A highly specific, sweet, artificial aromatic associated with synthetic vanilla extract.
panel.mixology: A simple, sweet, marshmallow-like vanilla flavor.

panel.origin: The fundamental taste of sweetness, primarily driven by sucrose retention post-roast.
panel.mixology: A general, pleasant sensation of sugar on the tongue.

panel.origin: An aromatic associated with the impression of a sweet substance without a specific character.
panel.mixology: A general sugary smell, like walking into a bakery or candy shop.

FLORAL.en: Floral notes — jasmine, rose, chamomile — are prized in light-roast specialty coffees from high-altitude, washed-process beans; they fade quickly with darker roasting.
FLORAL.es: Las notas florales — jazmín, rosa, manzanilla — son apreciadas en cafés de especialidad de tueste claro provenientes de granos de proceso lavado y gran altitud; se desvanecen rápido con un tueste más oscuro.

FRUITY.en: Fruity notes — berries, citrus, stone fruit — come from sugars and acids in the coffee cherry, and are most pronounced in natural or honey-processed coffees and lighter roasts.
FRUITY.es: Las notas afrutadas — bayas, cítricos, fruta de hueso — provienen de los azúcares y ácidos de la cereza del café, y son más pronunciadas en los procesos naturales o de miel y en los tuestes claros.

SOUR/FERMENTED.en: Sour and fermented notes can be GOOD (bright, citric, winey — careful processing) or a FAULT (vinegar, rotten fruit — cherries that over-fermented before drying).
SOUR/FERMENTED.es: Las notas ácidas y fermentadas pueden ser BUENAS (brillantes, cítricas, vinosas — un procesamiento cuidadoso) o un DEFECTO (vinagre, fruta podrida — cerezas que se sobrefermentaron antes del secado).

GREEN/VEGETATIVE.en: Green and vegetative notes — grassy, peapod, hay — usually signal underripe cherries or a light, underdeveloped roast.
GREEN/VEGETATIVE.es: Las notas verdes y vegetales — pasto, vaina de chícharo, heno — suelen señalar cerezas inmaduras o un tueste claro y poco desarrollado.

OTHER.en: This group covers FAULTS and off-notes — papery, musty, medicinal, rubbery — that point to storage problems, defective beans, or contamination.
OTHER.es: Este grupo cubre DEFECTOS y notas extrañas — papel, humedad, medicinal, hule — que apuntan a problemas de almacenamiento, granos defectuosos o contaminación.

ROASTED.en: Roasted notes — smoke, ash, tobacco, malt — come directly from the roasting process itself; a heavier roasted character usually means a darker roast.
ROASTED.es: Las notas tostadas — humo, ceniza, tabaco, malta — provienen directamente del propio proceso de tostado; un carácter tostado más intenso suele indicar un tueste más oscuro.

SPICES.en: Spice notes — cinnamon, clove, pepper — are subtle aromatic compounds that show up most clearly in medium roasts, before being masked by heavier roast character.
SPICES.es: Las notas de especia — canela, clavo, pimienta — son compuestos aromáticos sutiles que se notan más claramente en los tuestes medios, antes de quedar enmascarados por un carácter de tueste más intenso.

NUTTY/COCOA.en: Nutty and cocoa notes — almond, hazelnut, dark chocolate — are classic markers of a well-balanced medium roast, especially from Central and South American coffees.
NUTTY/COCOA.es: Las notas de frutos secos y cacao — almendra, avellana, chocolate amargo — son marcadores clásicos de un tueste medio bien equilibrado, especialmente en cafés de Centro y Sudamérica.

SWEET.en: Sweet notes — caramel, honey, vanilla — come from sugars caramelizing during roasting (the Maillard reaction) and are a hallmark of high-quality, well-roasted coffee.
SWEET.es: Las notas dulces — caramelo, miel, vainilla — provienen de los azúcares que se caramelizan durante el tostado (la reacción de Maillard) y son un sello de un café de alta calidad bien tostado.
